name: M.S. in Applied Mathematics

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: Only one course was parsed for this 30-credit M.S., the 0-credit MATH 791 Graduate Seminar (referencedCourses 1, requirementGroups 0), so the analysis/numerical-methods/modeling progression that an applied-math degree should show is entirely invisible on the page.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the applied-mathematics core (e.g., analysis, numerical methods, ODE/PDE, and modeling) with its intended sequence so coherence can be seen.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: deepestPrereqChain is 0 and the only referenced course is a seminar with no prerequisites, so no prerequisite structure is available to evaluate; missingCourseCount 0 is vacuous here.
dimensions.suggestion: List course prerequisites so the foundational-to-advanced progression (e.g., toward numerical methods and PDEs) is legible.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or objectives are present in the data.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish learning outcomes (e.g., formulate and analyze mathematical models, implement numerical methods, communicate quantitative results).

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits is 30, a conventional and transparent total, but with only the 0-credit seminar parsed and requirementGroups 0 the split across core, electives, and a thesis/project is invisible, and creditReconcileDelta is null.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the credit distribution across required core, electives, and thesis/project so the 30 credits are accounted for.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The only visible course is MATH 791, whose excerpt is an administrative registration note ('1 contact hour... must register each semester'); there is no program description, course list, or plan of study.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a program description and a course list with a plan of study so the page conveys the degree's substance beyond the seminar.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: Currency cannot be judged from a single seminar entry; the parsed data shows none of the computational, optimization, or data-driven modeling content that defines contemporary applied mathematics.
dimensions.suggestion: Surface the actual course list so modern emphases (scientific computing, optimization, machine-learning-adjacent modeling) are visible and assessable.

descriptionQuality.justification: The single parsed course (MATH 791) carries only an administrative seminar note rather than substantive academic content, so there is effectively no course-description quality to evaluate for the degree.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Only the seminar was parsed and it has no prerequisites; missingCourseCount 0 indicates no dangling references, but there is essentially nothing to assess for prerequisite clarity.

missingTopics.topic: Numerical linear algebra and scientific computing
missingTopics.rationale: A cornerstone of applied mathematics; the department clearly teaches MATH 611 Numerical Methods for Computation, but no computational core is visible in this program's parsed data.
Optimization underpins most modern applications and should be an explicit part of an applied-math M.S.

missingTopics.topic: Partial differential equations and mathematical modeling
missingTopics.rationale: PDE-based modeling is the applied-math backbone and is not surfaced in the catalog data.
Applied mathematics increasingly intersects statistical/ML methods; an elective bridging to the department's statistics courses would strengthen the degree.
Stochastic methods (the department offers MATH 605 Stochastic Calculus) are central to applied modeling but not shown here.

emergingTrends.trend: Scientific machine learning / physics-informed neural networks
emergingTrends.rationale: Blending numerical PDEs with deep learning is a fast-growing applied-math research frontier.

emergingTrends.trend: Data-driven dynamical systems and model reduction
emergingTrends.rationale: Techniques like DMD and reduced-order modeling are reshaping computational modeling.
Large-scale simulation increasingly depends on parallel/GPU methods that a modern curriculum should teach.
Quantifying model and data uncertainty is now expected in simulation-based science and engineering.
Optimization at scale is a bridge between applied math and modern AI applications.

topSuggestions: Publish the full course list and plan of study; the page currently exposes only the 0-credit MATH 791 seminar.
Surface a computational core (numerical methods, optimization, PDE modeling) so the degree's applied focus is visible.
Make the 30-credit distribution explicit across core, electives, and thesis/project.
Add a scientific-machine-learning or data-driven-modeling elective to signal currency.
